name: Sunrise Restaurant Hawaii
tagline: Okinawan-style home cooking on Kapahulu — a cuisine you can't find outside Hawaii or Okinawa itself.
mustOrder: Okinawan soba — different from mainland Japanese soba, thicker and heartier
goya champuru — bitter melon stir-fry, the classic Okinawan dish
parking: Street parking on Kapahulu Ave; small lot nearby
talkStory: Hawaii has the largest Okinawan diaspora outside Japan, and Sunrise has been keeping that culinary heritage alive on Kapahulu since the 1980s. Family-run, serving home-style Okinawan dishes you literally cannot find outside Okinawa or Hawaii — goya champuru, Okinawan soba, and fresh andagi doughnuts. The crowd is mostly older Okinawan and Japanese families. A deeply cultural pick that connects to Hawaii's immigration story. Lunch and dinner, closed Mondays.
